questions.question_text: Natural fats are composed of
questions.explanation: Fats consist of the monomers or smaller molecules, glycerol and fatty acid. These molecules are composed of the chemical elements hydrogen, carbon, and oxygen.

questions.question_text:
Which of the following blood types are safe to use for a person with type B blood?

I. A

II. B

III. O
questions.explanation: O is a universal donor and all blood groups can receive blood group O . Blood group B can take blood from B group.

questions.question_text:
Which of the following pollutants cause(s) acid rain?

I. CO 2 

II. NO 2 

III. SO 3
questions.explanation: Acid rain is caused by the release of Sulphur oxides (SO 2 and SO 3 ) and nitrogen oxides into the atmosphere, where they react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to become sulfuric and nitric acid respectively.

When anaerobic respiration and aerobic respiration are compared, the energy yield
questions.explanation: Aerobic processes produce up to 38 ATP per glucose. Anaerobic processes yield only 2 ATP per glucose

questions.question_text: An example of a unisexual flower is
questions.explanation: Unisexual flowers : Coconut flowers, Papaya, Watermelon, Cucumber, Maize, White mulberry, Musk melon,Castor bean, Marrow, Luffa, Snake gourd, Bitter gourd, Tapioca, Pumpkin, American holly, Birch, Pine, Gopher purge, Tungoil bean.

questions.question_text: Which of the following materials is non-magnetic?
questions.explanation: Materials that can be magnetized, which are also the ones that are strongly attracted to a magnet, are called ferromagnetic (or ferrimagnetic). These include the elements iron, nickel and cobalt and their alloys, some alloys of rare-earth metals, and some naturally occurring minerals such as lodesto

questions.question_text:

I. anti-rust properties.

II. relative cheapness.

III. ability to be moulded into shapes.

Which of the statements above are correct ?
Species, genus and family are examples of
questions.explanation: A taxon (plural taxa ) is a unit of classification. The examples of taxa at different hierarchical levels are species, followed by genus, family, order, class, phylum (in animals) or division (in plants), and kingdom, in ascending order.

questions.explanation: There are 2 out of 4 (half) Aa , 1 out of 4 of AA and 1 out of 4 of aa

questions.question_text: Sound waves travel fastest in
questions.explanation: Sound wave travels fastest in solids, less in liquids and least in gases. It cannot travel in vacuum as it is a mechanical wave which require material medium for propagation.
